'''Jack Owens''' (born 3 June 1994) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a [[Goal (sports)#Rugby football|goal]]-kicking {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|wg}} for the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Owens was born in [[Ditton, Cheshire|Ditton]], a residential area of [[Widnes]], [[Cheshire, England]].

==Career==
Having previously played for the [[Widnes Vikings]], he was released from the club on 27 October 2015. His début for the Vikings came in a [[Challenge Cup]] match against Siddal and his league début was against [[Barrow Raiders]] in May 2011.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/runcorn-widnes-news/runcorn-widnes-local-news/2011/05/26/widnes-vikings-debut-joy-for-sweet-sixteen-owens-55368-28773310|title=Widnes Vikings: début joy for sweet sixteen Owens|newspaper=Runcorn and Widnes Weekly News|date=26 May 2011|accessdate=4 April 2013}}</ref> He officially joined [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in November 2015, for the 2016 season.

During the 2017 season the {{rlp|fb}} joined [[Kingstone Press Championship|Championship]] side [[Sheffield Eagles]] on a loan spell that lasted until the end of the season.

He joined newly relegated [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[Kingstone Press Championship|Championship]] ahead of the 2018 season having been released by the Saints.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41679942|title=Jack Owens: Leigh Centurions sign former St Helens full-back|publisher=BBC Sport|date=19 October 2017|accessdate=24 January 2018}}</ref>
